---
title: "My first 10K"
date: 2011-02-12
date-modified: 2026-03-08
author: Jonathan Whitmore
draft: true
description: "A personal account of running the Death Valley Borax 10K race, including a GPS-tracked Google Earth flythrough video of the course."
categories: [running, personal]
---

[caption id="" align="alignright" width="300" caption="Image via Wikipedia"][![Dunes at Death Valley, Ca looking east towards...](http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ef/Quietdunes.jpg/300px-Quietdunes.jpg)](http://commons.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Quietdunes.jpg)[/caption]


So, my first half marathon turned into my first 10K race: The Death Valley Borax 10K. A few other friends I talked into running came with me and in the end Stephanie and Jeff raced and completed the half marathon, while Kelsey kindly ran the 10K with me.

Death Valley is a beautiful place -- and in the early morning in February, it's a great temperature. I had a great time (as in fun, not finish).

I ran with my [Forerunner 305](http://amzn.to/hd1vhi) and I exported the GPS data and used Google Earth to run the same route -- so you could enjoy following along!

{{< video http://www.youtube.com/embed/GJ9aEWkjhMA >}}
